title: Parallax blindfold slice-scope manifest (v0.37.3 F1)
description: Per-slice input to scripts/blindfold-guard.py --scope-manifest (monorepo mode). SLICE-SPECIFIC by construction, never a whole-repo allowlist: it names the slice's OWN new/changed implementation/test paths (which always fail closed when visible to the opposite blind track, before any allowlist) and the sibling-package roots the test-writer side may see for cross-package import resolution only. Written by /parallax:run per slice at .parallax/<slug>/blindfold-scope.<slice_id>.json, derived from slices.md/slices.lock plus validation.md's 'Monorepo dependency roots' line. A whole-tree dependency glob ('**' or any all-wildcard variant) is rejected here at the schema level — monorepo mode must never degrade into a blanket --allow-glob '**' bypass.

protected_impl_paths.description: The slice's OWN new/changed implementation files (exact repo-relative paths, not globs). Contamination on the test side whenever tracked — checked before --allow-glob and before dependency_allow_globs, so no allowlist can mask them.

protected_test_paths.description: The slice's OWN test files (exact repo-relative paths, not globs). Contamination on the code side whenever tracked, in addition to the guard's normal test heuristics.

dependency_allow_globs.description: Resolvable sibling-package roots the TEST side may see for import resolution only (e.g. 'packages/shared/src/**', 'packages/shared/dist/**'). Must stay package-specific — mechanically: the FIRST path segment must be a fully literal directory name (letters/digits/._@- , not starting with a dot), with no fnmatch wildcard ('*', '?', '[…]') anywhere in it. That kills every whole-tree-equivalent form ('**', '**/*', '*', '*.*', './**', '/**', '?*', '[a-z]*', …) while allowing real package roots like 'packages/*/dist/**' (literal 'packages' first, wildcards only below it). A whole-tree allowlist is not an acceptable monorepo mode.
